blurb:
Beignet's story started with Good Mews as a kitten, took a rough detour through a county shelter, and now she's back where she belongs. Nobody knows what happened in between, but she showed up scared and alone until the shelter called Good Mews to bring her home. She's been in foster care getting her confidence back, and it's working.

She's sweet once she warms up and loves being petted. She's one of those cats who likes company when she eats, happily munching away when you're nearby. When she wants attention, she'll sit close or lean against you. She can give gentle love bites when she gets overstimulated, so you'll want to watch her signals and give her space when she needs it.

This is her second chance, and she's ready for someone who gets cats and won't rush her. Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road. You'll need to submit an inquiry and visit in person to meet her.

Good Mews Animal Foundation
3805 Robinson Road, Marietta, GA 30067
(770) 499-2287 | adopt@goodmews.org
scoring_notes:
• Compelling comeback story from scared shelter cat to recovering foster pet
• Second chance narrative creates emotional connection with readers
• Extended foster care suggests longer wait time and reader urgency

blurb:
His name is Pork Tenderloin, which is either the best cat name ever or someone at the shelter has a serious sense of humor. Either way, he's a handsome guy who came in from a county shelter looking for a better shot at finding his people.

He's sweet but likes attention on his own terms. He'll come find you when he's in the mood for affection, but he's not going to be all over you constantly. He can get overwhelmed more easily than some cats, so he needs someone who understands when he's had enough and will give him space to decompress.

This isn't a cat for someone who wants to pick him up and carry him around. He's looking for a cat-savvy person who reads feline body language and respects boundaries. If that's you, he has the potential to form a really special bond. Good Mews is on Robinson Road in Marietta. Submit an inquiry to get started.

Good Mews Animal Foundation
3805 Robinson Road, Marietta, GA 30067
(770) 499-2287 | adopt@goodmews.org
scoring_notes:
• Memorable name creates instant reader engagement and shareability
• Requires experienced owner which limits appeal to general audience
• Story lacks compelling backstory or urgency indicators

blurb:
Luke's story starts with heartbreak. His owner died unexpectedly, and not one family member would help Miss Daisy and her two puppies. The boyfriend dropped them all at the county shelter and walked away. They sat there for weeks waiting for someone to care enough to come get them.

Luckily, Our Pal's Place stepped in right when their summer camp was happening, so Luke got tons of attention from kids who taught him that people can be good. He's a fun-loving, playful shepherd mix who looks more like a Belgian Malinois than his German Shepherd mom. He's also on the smaller side, which makes him perfect if you want shepherd energy without the full-size commitment.

Luke's had a rough start, but he's still just a young, loving boy who wants to play and trust again. He's gotten to experience what it's like to run around outside and go on trail walks. Now he just needs someone to show him that this is how life is supposed to be.

Our Pal's Place is on Canton Road in Marietta. Contact them to meet Luke and see if you're the family he's been waiting for.
scoring_notes:
• Tragic family abandonment story creates maximum emotional impact
• Young puppy with deceased owner tugs heartstrings of all readers
• Extended shelter stay with family rejection shows desperate need

blurb:
Otis was on the county shelter's kill list just because of bad timing. When the rescue team found him, he was so stressed from the chaos that he was urinating on himself. But the second they got him into a quiet room, he buried his head in someone's lap and wouldn't let go. He just needed out.

Now that he's at Our Pal's Place, the real Otis has come back. He's house-trained again, sits politely, and his tail actually wags. He loves attention and wants nothing more than to make his humans happy. The volunteers say he's turned into this fun-loving guy who's eager to please.

Whoever gets Otis is going to have a dog who knows what it's like to almost lose everything. He's not going to take his new home for granted. He's the kind of dog who will follow you around just because he can't believe his luck.

Our Pal's Place is on Canton Road in Marietta. You can visit during their adoption hours or reach out to start the process.
scoring_notes:
• Dramatic rescue from kill list creates powerful emotional appeal
• Transformation story shows clear before/after for reader satisfaction
• Well-behaved and grateful personality makes adoption feel rewarding

blurb:
Tiny lives up to her name. She's small and delicate, but she's got curiosity in spades. She came to Good Mews from a county shelter for a better chance at finding someone, and she's been making the most of her time there exploring every corner she can find.

She's playful without being crazy about it. She'll chase a toy when the mood hits, investigate new spots, then settle in for some cozy time when she's done. She brings a gentle energy to whatever she's doing, which is nice in a cat. Not bouncing off the walls, just engaged and interested in her world.

She's the kind of cat who would do well with someone looking for a companion who's both affectionate and entertaining. Small cats often have big personalities, and Tiny seems to fit that pattern. Good Mews is a cage-free shelter on Robinson Road. You'll need to submit an inquiry and visit to meet her.

Good Mews Animal Foundation
3805 Robinson Road, Marietta, GA 30067
(770) 499-2287 | adopt@goodmews.org
scoring_notes:
• Appealing personality and size make her accessible to most adopters
• Small cat with big personality angle has broad reader appeal
• Generic rescue story lacks emotional hook or time urgency

blurb:
Junior lived in the same home for years until his family suddenly decided they were allergic to him. That's the reason they gave anyway when they dropped him at the county shelter. He's a black Lab, so people kept walking past his kennel. Black dog syndrome is real and it hit Junior hard.

But here's what makes Junior special: this dog has a standing ice cream date. He loves going to Bruster's and Pelicans, and when he's done with his treat, he goes home with one of the volunteers. At home, he's everything you want in a dog. House-trained, well-mannered, loving. He just wants to be someone's only dog and hang out with older kids who won't overwhelm him.

Junior knows what it's like to be thrown away after years of loyalty. He's not going to mess around with his second chance. If you're looking for a devoted companion who will never take you for granted, this is your guy.

Our Pal's Place is on Canton Road in Marietta. Reach out to meet Junior and maybe plan that first ice cream trip together.
scoring_notes:
• Heartbreaking abandonment story plus black dog syndrome creates urgency
• Ice cream dates provide charming, shareable newsletter content
• Years of loyalty betrayed resonates with readers seeking devoted pets
